content.pattern: Working on leo-core using leo-core itself (dogfooding) requires extra care. This repo contains the Go CLI (cli/), the KB (.claude/kb/), and the CLAUDE.md bootloader. Changes to KB docs propagate to downstream projects via `leo update` (issue #26).
content.when_to_use: Every session where Leo is working inside the leo-core repo itself (self_hosting: true in project-config.yml)
how: This repo is three things: (1) the Go CLI source in cli/, (2) the core KB in .claude/kb/, (3) the CLAUDE.md bootloader. Understand which you're editing and why

KB changes propagate to downstream projects when the owner runs `leo update` in those projects. Not instant like the old symlink model, but still impactful — escalation-triggers rule applies. Leo never edits a rule without R2
Editing Leo mid-session: the current session keeps old version in memory. Open new session to validate behavior changes
No other projects to escalate to: meta-work is not a license for direct mode. Think-before-execute and escalation-triggers still apply without exception
Core artifacts locked to English: enforced via project-config.yml locales.project_files: en
Recommended flow: owner describes intent → Leo applies think-before-execute → structural decision = alignment mode → proposal for R2 → approved → edit → evidence → wrap-up on owner signal
